overview.description: Many effects impose a condition, a temporary state that alters the recipient's capabilities
overview.stacking_rule: Conditions don't stack - either you have a condition or you don't
overview.exception: Exhaustion condition effects get worse with multiple instances

duration_rules.specified_duration: Lasts for the duration specified by the imposing effect
duration_rules.until_countered: Lasts until the condition is countered
duration_rules.multiple_instances: Each instance has its own duration, but effects don't worsen (except Exhaustion)

blinded.name: Blinded
effects: Can't see and automatically fails ability checks that require sight
Attack rolls have Disadvantage
Attack rolls against you have Advantage

charmed.name: Charmed
effects: Can't attack the charmer or target them with harmful abilities or magical effects
The charmer has Advantage on ability checks to interact socially with you

deafened.name: Deafened
effects: Can't hear and automatically fails ability checks that require hearing

exhaustion.name: Exhaustion

3.effect: Disadvantage on attack rolls and saving throws

exhaustion.recovery: One level reduced by finishing a long rest (if some food and drink consumed)

frightened.name: Frightened
effects: Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls while the source of fear is within line of sight
Can't willingly move closer to the source of fear

grappled.name: Grappled
effects: Speed becomes 0, and you can't benefit from any bonus to speed
Condition ends if the grappler is incapacitated
Condition ends if an effect removes you from the reach of the grappler
escape_options: Use action to escape with Athletics or Acrobatics check vs grappler's Athletics
Effect moves you out of grappler's reach

incapacitated.name: Incapacitated
effects: Can't take actions or reactions

invisible.name: Invisible
effects: Impossible to see without the aid of magic or a special sense
Considered heavily obscured for the purpose of hiding
Attack rolls have Advantage
Attack rolls against you have Disadvantage

paralyzed.name: Paralyzed
effects: Incapacitated and can't move or speak
Automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws
Attack rolls against you have Advantage
Any attack that hits you is a critical hit if the attacker is within 5 feet

petrified.name: Petrified
effects: Transformed, along with any nonmagical objects worn or carried, into a solid inanimate substance (usually stone)
Weight increases by a factor of ten, and ceases aging
Incapacitated, can't move or speak, and is unaware of surroundings
Automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws
Has resistance to all damage
Immune to poison and disease (existing poison and disease effects are suspended)

poisoned.name: Poisoned
effects: Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks

prone.name: Prone
effects: Can only crawl or teleport to move (crawling costs extra movement)
Disadvantage on attack rolls
Attack rolls against you have Advantage if attacker is within 5 feet
Attack rolls against you have Disadvantage if attacker is more than 5 feet away
prone.counter: Use half your movement to stand up
prone.tactical_considerations: Ranged attackers have Disadvantage, melee attackers have Advantage

restrained.name: Restrained
effects: Speed becomes 0, and you can't benefit from any bonus to speed
Attack rolls have Disadvantage
Attack rolls against you have Advantage
Disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws

stunned.name: Stunned
effects: Incapacitated, can't move, and can speak only falteringly
Automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws
Attack rolls against you have Advantage

unconscious.name: Unconscious
effects: Incapacitated, can't move or speak, and is unaware of surroundings
Drops whatever it's holding and falls prone
Automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws
Attack rolls against you have Advantage
Any attack that hits you is a critical hit if the attacker is within 5 feet

stacking_rules.general_rule: Multiple instances of the same condition don't stack
stacking_rules.duration_tracking: Each instance tracks its own duration separately
stacking_rules.exhaustion_exception: Exhaustion levels stack and effects worsen

condition_immunity.description: Some creatures have immunity to specific conditions

examples.constructs: Often immune to charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned
examples.undead: Often immune to charmed, exhaustion, poisoned
examples.elementals: Varies by type, often immune to several conditions

offensive_applications.setup_combos: Apply conditions to enable critical hits or advantage
offensive_applications.battlefield_control: Use area conditions to restrict enemy movement
offensive_applications.resource_drain: Force enemies to spend actions/resources removing conditions
